assessment_items.question_content:
**The cache of gold objects found in Mycenaean tombs is an especially important source for understanding Greek bronze age culture since very little writing from the period exists.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The gave goods from the tombs provide cultural evidence that helps scholars to better understand Mycenaean culture.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/agamemnon-mask)**

**Gold masks like these were attached to the face of the deceased and other gold objects surrounded the bodies found in these grave circles.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/agamemnon-mask)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the ancient Aegean period?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Few examples of ancient Aegean writing survive today.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/agamemnon-mask)**

choices.content: The Ancient Aegean period predates Greek culture and gave rise to many Greek legends.
The three most significant cultures during the Ancient Aegean period were the Cycladic, Minoan, and Mycenaean civilizations.
Because of the fortified citadel and weapons, some scholars speculate Mycenae was  militaristic.
The Ancient Aegeans did not have written language.

hints:
**Ancient Aegean merchants traded goods from Spain to the Near East.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/mycenae-palace)**

choices.content: only locally among the people of ancient Greece.
across the mediterranean and the near East.
with no one beyond their borders.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Mycenaeans built monumental domed tombs known as . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22667d84133ad5731b5cf54649f1710e.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Mycenaeans built monumental domed tombs for their ruling elite. These tombs are called tholos or beehive tombs.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/Treasury-of-Atreus)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The sophisticated engineering of the ancient Mycenaeans allowed them to build the domed Treasury of Atreus, which was the largest dome in the world for more than a thousand years.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The Treasury of Atreus was the largest dome in the world until the Pantheon in Rome was built more than 1000 years later.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/Treasury-of-Atreus)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The unadorned facade of the Treasury of Atreus is an expression of the austere style of the Mycenaeans.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Treasury of Atreus was once highly decorated with decorative motifs and other carvings, fragments of which can be found in the Archaeological Museum in Athens and in The British Museum.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The masonry that lines the dromos is known as . . . **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3222fabf6db1ff7ee0b682e6da6d8c37.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The masonry that lines the dromos (entrance pathway) is known as cyclopean.**
